original_image_tags: A rectangular, off-white paper envelope fragment, horizontally oriented, is mounted on a dark, textured background. The fragment is torn irregularly on the right side, with the original right edge and back flap missing. The top edge shows a slight curve. The fragment is secured under a clear plastic or glassine overlay, visible by reflections and tape residue on the right and bottom edges. The background appears to be black or dark grey cardstock.
A brown 3-cent U.S. postage stamp, partially torn on the right edge. The stamp depicts three children in profile, wearing hats (possibly a Boy Scout theme), with text visible.

elements.description: A photograph of a torn, aged envelope with handwritten address and a partial postage stamp. The envelope is addressed to 'MIDWEST NEWS, 3435 W. CHICAGO AVE., CHICAGO, ILL.' and features a circular postmark reading 'CHICAGO, ILL. JUL 25 6 PM 1950' and a partially torn brown 3-cent U.S. postage stamp depicting three figures. A handwritten number '62-83894-292' is visible at the bottom of the envelope, and a large faint 'K' is written on the left side.

judge.reason: Description B adds specific quoted text (address, postmark, handwritten number '62-83894-292', large faint 'K') that Description A does not mention at all, creating a factual inconsistency on the presence of visual content.
